communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request the access to and copies of all records related to: 
- All stop arm enforcement contracts and amendments
- The total number of buses in their fleet;
- The total number of buses serving special needs students; 
- The total number of buses installed with a stop arm camera system;
- The total number of buses serving special needs students installed with a stop arm camera system

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

title: Economic Development Incentives for Hadrian Automation, Inc.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Alabama Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

(1)	Any and all applications, contracts, or authorizations of economic development/tax incentives, tax credits, or grants for Hadrian Automation, Inc., AE Industrial Partners, LP, and/or Rocket Shoals, LLC. These may include but are not limited to a Growing Alabama credit, an investment credit, and an Industrial Development Grant under the State Industrial Development Authority.
(2)	Any and all applications, contracts, or authorizations of economic development grants for Colbert County. These may include but are not limited to a Shoals Economic Development Fund (SEDF) grant.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

André Natta, a citizen of Alabama, in coordination with Julia Gledhill.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act / applicable state public records law, I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera / school bus infraction detection system associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFQ# EPSD.DISTRICT.2025.0001

Title/Description: Stop Arm Surveillance for Bus Fleet 

Issuing Entity: East Providence School District

Specifically, I am requesting: 

The Total Number of Buses Installed?

Have their been any citations issued?

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith
